label: Employment
Mlstr_harmo::algorithm:
occupation_level <- case_when(
  occupst_1 %in% 1 | occupst_2 %in% 1 ~ 1L, 
  occupst_1 %in% 2 | occupst_2 %in% 2 ~ 2L, 
  occupst_1 %in% 4 & occupst_2 %in% 4 ~ 3L, 
  occupst_1 %in% 5 & occupst_2 %in% 5 ~ 4L,        
  is.na(occupst_1) & occupst_2 %in% 5 ~ 4L, 
  is.na(occupst_2) & occupst_1 %in% 5 ~ 4L,  
  (occupst_1 %in% 4 & occupst_2 %in% 3) | (occupst_1 %in% 3 & occupst_2 %in% 4)  ~ 6L,
  (occupst_1 %in% 4 & occupst_2 %in% 5) | (occupst_1 %in% 5 & occupst_2 %in% 4) ~ 4L, 
  (occupst_1 %in% 4 & occupst_2 %in% 6) | (occupst_1 %in% 6 & occupst_2 %in% 4) ~ 7L,
  occupst_1 %in% c(7,8,9) & occupst_2 %in% c(7,8,9) ~ 5L, 
  is.na(occupst_1) & occupst_2 %in% c(7,8,9) ~ 5L,
  is.na(occupst_2) & occupst_1 %in% c(7,8,9) ~ 5L,  
  (occupst_1 %in% 3 & occupst_2 %in% 5) | (occupst_1 %in% 5 & occupst_2 %in% 3) ~ 4L,     
  (occupst_1 %in% 6 & occupst_2 %in% 5) | (occupst_1 %in% 5 & occupst_2 %in% 6) ~ 7L,   
  occupst_1 %in% 3 & occupst_2 %in% 3 ~ 6L,   
  is.na(occupst_1) & occupst_2 %in% 3 ~ 6L,   
  is.na(occupst_2) & occupst_1 %in% 3 ~ 6L,   
  (occupst_1 %in% 3 & occupst_2 %in% 6) | (occupst_1 %in% 6 & occupst_2 %in% 3) ~ 7L, 
  occupst_1 %in% c(6,10) | occupst_2 %in% c(6,10) ~ 7L,         
  is.na(occupst_1) & is.na(occupst_2) ~ 7L,                                   occupst_1 %in% 10 & occupst_2 %in% 10 ~ 7L,                          occupst_1 %in% 10 & occupst_2 %in% c(1:9) ~ 1L,                    occupst_1 %in% c(1:9) & occupst_2 %in% 10 ~ 1L,                     
  TRUE ~ NA_integer_
)
Mlstr_harmo::comment:
# If either parent is "fully employed (100%)," assign 1L.
# If either parent works "more than half day," "half day," or "less than half day,"  assign 2L.
# If both parents are "are home markers," assign 3L.                                                                   # If both parents are "retired," assign 4L.                                                                  # If both parents are "not at all employed," assign 5L.
# If one parent's status is NA, use the other parent's status to determine the category. # If one parent's status is lower, use the other parent's higher-status to determine the category. 
# If both are NA, assign 7L ("other").
